source: Autonomous web research by Chief Decifer
synthesis: Across 2023-2025 research, the clearest pattern is that multi-factor signal models consistently outperform single-factor approaches, but only when factor weights are dynamically calibrated using Information Coefficient (IC) tracking rather than equal-weighting — IC-dynamic strategies show returns up to 4x higher than static alternatives. A second critical finding is regime-dependency: momentum dominates in low-volatility bull markets while mean reversion is most effective in high-volatility or bear regimes, making regime detection the master gate for which sub-signals get amplified. Third, signal half-life is real and measurable — US equity momentum signals decay ~60% within the first 10 months and alpha erodes at 5.6% annually, meaning Decifer's 9-dimension score needs a formal freshness/decay audit layer or it will quietly degrade without anyone noticing. On alternative data: SEC Form 4 insider cluster buying (multiple insiders buying same week) carries a robust, free-to-access 5.2% 6-month alpha edge, while WSB Reddit sentiment alone generates no reliable alpha but adds confirming signal value when combined with technicals. Options flow unusual activity (volume > open interest) is academically confirmed as selectively predictive — the specific conditions (large, short-dated, OTM contracts) matter enormously.
top_3_quick_wins: IC-Weighted Signal Scorer — Replace equal-weighted 9-dimension score with rolling Information Coefficient weights per dimension
SEC Form 4 Insider Cluster Dimension — Add a 10th signal dimension detecting multi-insider buying clusters via free EDGAR RSS
Signal Decay Monitor — Track per-dimension IC rolling mean and flag decaying signals before they drag composite score quality

findings.expected_impact: +3-6% annualized alpha vs equal-weighted baseline; IC-dynamic weighting shown to produce returns 4x higher than static weighting in peer-reviewed 2025 study
findings.summary: Replace Decifer's current equal-weight 9-dimension signal composite with a rolling Information Coefficient (IC) weighting system that re-ranks each dimension by its recent predictive power against actual trade outcomes. IC measures the correlation between a signal's score and the subsequent return — even a small positive IC of 0.05-0.10 applied consistently across many trades compounds into significant alpha. This is directly applicable to Decifer's existing signal architecture since trade outcomes are already logged.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the 9-dimension composite scorer from fixed equal-weights to evidence-driven adaptive weights
findings.what_changes: Add a rolling IC calculator (Spearman rank correlation between each dimension's score and 5-day forward return) computed weekly on the trade journal. Weight each of the 9 dimensions by its ICIR (IC mean / IC std) before summing into final composite score. Dimensions with negative or near-zero IC are down-weighted automatically.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer currently has no mechanism to detect which of its 9 signal dimensions are actually working vs. contributing noise. In paper trading, every live trade is a free data point — this feature mines those data points to improve itself continuously. Critical for the transition from paper to live trading where every basis point counts.
findings.source_evidence: arxiv.org/html/2508.18592v1 — 'Combined machine learning for stock selection strategy based on dynamic weighting methods' (Aug 2025): IC-based dynamic weighting outperforms static methods with returns nearly 4x higher. Also supported by MSCI Barra IC framework (Grinold 1994) and CFA L3 quantitative investing curriculum.

findings.feature: SEC Form 4 Insider Cluster Buying Dimension
findings.expected_impact: +4.8-5.2% 6-month alpha on triggered trades; opportunistic cluster buys (multiple insiders, same 10-day window) are the highest-conviction version of this signal
findings.summary: Add a 10th signal dimension that polls the SEC EDGAR RSS feed (free, no API key required) for Form 4 insider purchase filings on Decifer's watchlist symbols. The signal fires only on 'cluster' conditions — 2+ independent insiders (CEO, CFO, director) purchasing within 10 days — which academic research identifies as the high-conviction subset. Insider selling is explicitly excluded as it has weak predictive power due to diversification/tax motivations.
findings.upgrades: Adds a new 10th dimension to the signal scoring system, expanding the alternative data layer beyond Yahoo RSS news
findings.what_changes: Poll https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&type=4&dateb=&owner=include&count=10&search_text= for each symbol in the watchlist. Parse Form 4 XML for transaction type P (purchase) vs. S (sale). Cluster score = number of unique insiders buying in last 10 days. Score 0 if 0-1 insiders, 5 if 2 insiders, 10 if 3+ insiders. Feed into brain loop as a 0-10 dimension score.
findings.why_it_matters: SEC EDGAR is completely free and legally open. This gives Decifer a genuine institutional-quality alternative data signal that costs zero dollars. During paper trading it builds a track record of whether cluster detection predicts positive outcomes on Decifer's specific watchlist, giving us ground-truth data before live deployment.
findings.source_evidence: MarketTriage.com analysis of Lakonishok & Lee (2002, Review of Financial Studies): 4.8% 12-month alpha from heavy insider buying. Cohen, Malloy & Pomorski (2012, Journal of Finance): opportunistic cluster purchases show 5.2% 6-month alpha. ScienceDirect 2024 study 'Insider filings as trading signals — Does it pay to be fast?' confirms predictive power persists post-Sarbanes-Oxley.

findings.expected_impact: Prevents silent degradation of composite score quality; early detection of decaying dimensions estimated to preserve 3-5% annualized alpha that would otherwise erode
findings.summary: Build a scheduled weekly process that computes rolling IC for each of Decifer's 9 signal dimensions over the trailing 60-trade window, and flags any dimension where IC has dropped below 0.02 (near-zero predictive power) or gone negative. US equity momentum signals are shown to decay ~60% within 10 months at 5.6% annually — without monitoring, Decifer could be running degraded signals for months before noticing in P&L. This is a defensive, high-leverage feature.
findings.upgrades: Adds observable signal health telemetry to the analytics layer — turns the trading journal into a live signal feedback loop
findings.what_changes: After each week of paper trading, join the signal score log with trade outcomes (5-day forward return). Compute Spearman IC for each dimension. Output a JSON report: {dimension: ic_value, trend: 'improving'|'stable'|'decaying'}. Alert via Telegram if any IC drops below 0.02 for 2 consecutive weeks. Display on local dashboard as a signal health panel.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer is in paper trading now — this is exactly the stage to build the measurement infrastructure that tells us if our signals are working. Alpha decay is well-documented (microalphas.com, 2025: 5.6% annual decay in US equities) and silent. Catching it early is a prerequisite for confident transition from paper to live trading.
findings.source_evidence: microalphas.com 'Signal Decay Analysis: Understanding Alpha Lifecycles' (June 2025): 5.6% annual alpha decay in US markets, momentum strategies go negative after ~10 months. optimization-online.org 'Multi-period portfolio optimization with alpha decay': short-term signals decay faster than long-term, requiring differentiated tracking. diva-portal.org LSTM alpha decay study 2024.

findings.feature: Regime-Conditional Signal Weighting (Momentum vs Mean-Reversion Gate)
findings.expected_impact: +2-4% win rate improvement by suppressing wrong-regime signals; momentum in bull/low-vol, mean-reversion in bear/high-vol
findings.summary: Implement a simple two-state market regime detector (using VIX proxy from yfinance and SPY 200-day MA) that dynamically gates which signal dimensions get amplified. Research clearly shows momentum dominates in low-volatility bull markets while mean reversion is more effective in high-volatility or transitional regimes. Rather than waiting for the backlog HMM Regime Detection (which is more complex), this is a lightweight rule-based gate that can be live in days.
findings.upgrades: Adds a regime-aware signal amplification layer that sits above the 9-dimension composite scorer
findings.what_changes: Each brain loop cycle: fetch SPY daily close + VIX via yfinance. If SPY > 200-day MA AND VIX < 20: BULL regime — multiply momentum/trend/breakout dimensions by 1.3, mean-reversion by 0.7. If SPY < 200-day MA OR VIX > 28: BEAR/HIGH-VOL regime — multiply mean-reversion/squeeze dimensions by 1.3, momentum by 0.7. Neutral otherwise. Log regime state with each signal score.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer has a known bullish bias and is currently in a single-regime environment assumption. Research from Price Action Lab (2024 backtesting over 2003-2025) shows a regime-switching ETF strategy achieves Sharpe 1.15 vs much lower for pure momentum or pure reversion. Decifer's current paper trading period is the ideal time to validate which regime state produces which outcomes.
findings.source_evidence: priceactionlab.com 'Mean-Reversion and Momentum Regime Switching' (Jan 2024): Sharpe 1.15 over 22-year backtest using regime gate; 10.3% annualized, 16.8% max drawdown. ResearchGate 2025 study on momentum vs mean reversion: 'Momentum dominates during bull markets, particularly low-volatility uptrends.' ScienceDirect regime-switching model (2023): semi-Markov model demonstrates short-term momentum followed by medium-term reversal.

findings.feature: Options Flow Proxy Signal via Put/Call Ratio
findings.expected_impact: +1-3% directional accuracy when options flow confirms or contradicts existing signal direction; strongest when used as confluence filter
findings.summary: Add a put/call ratio signal dimension using free data from yfinance options chains. A high put/call ratio (>1.2) is a sentiment-bearish signal; extreme ratios (>1.5) can act as a contrarian bullish indicator. Academic research (Journal of Portfolio Management) confirms certain unusual options activity conditions — particularly large, short-dated, OTM contracts with volume > open interest — are statistically associated with abnormal positive returns. This version uses free yfinance options data rather than paid flow feeds.
findings.upgrades: Adds options market intelligence to the flow dimension, upgrading it from pure technical to smart-money-aware
findings.what_changes: For each scored symbol, use yf.Ticker(symbol).options to fetch nearest expiry chain. Compute put_volume / call_volume ratio. Score: ratio < 0.7 = bullish (score 7-10), 0.7-1.0 = neutral (score 5), 1.0-1.3 = mild bearish (score 3-4), >1.3 = strong bearish or contrarian (score 1-2 directional, but flag for mean-reversion opportunity). Also flag if any single strike has volume > open_interest (unusual activity marker).
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer already has a 'flow' dimension — this concretely defines what that dimension should measure using free data. The put/call ratio is used by institutional traders as a primary sentiment gauge and is accessible for zero cost via yfinance. It gives Decifer's AI agents a real market-derived signal rather than just technical price-action.
findings.source_evidence: Wayne State University/Journal of Portfolio Management: 'Unusual options activity revealed as a potential return signal' — large, near-expiry, OTM options with high volume-to-OI show statistically significant positive abnormal returns. PMC/NIH 'Will the Reddit rebellion take you to the moon?' uses put-call ratio as primary sentiment proxy. unusualwhales.com practitioner guide: volume > OI is the primary UOA screen.

findings.expected_impact: Modest standalone alpha (WSB alone generates no reliable alpha per academic studies), but +1-2% win rate as a confluence accelerator when combined with technical signals
findings.summary: Upgrade the existing social signal dimension from qualitative to quantitative by tracking mention velocity (rate-of-change in mentions, not raw count) for watchlist symbols across Reddit. Academic research is clear: WSB sentiment alone does not generate tradeable alpha (the BUZZ ETF underperformed SPY by 15% over 2021-2024), but surge in mention velocity preceding a technical breakout is a reliable confluence amplifier. The signal is most valuable as a corroborating boost, not as a standalone trigger.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the social dimension from a binary news-exists flag to a quantitative velocity score
findings.what_changes: Use Reddit's free JSON API (reddit.com/r/wallstreetbets/search.json?q=TICKER&sort=new&limit=100) to count symbol mentions in the last 6 hours vs. 24-hour baseline. Velocity score = (6h_mentions / hourly_baseline - 1). Score 0-10: <0 = no buzz, 0-2x = 3, 2-5x = 6, >5x = 9. Only contribute positively to composite score if technical signals (trend + momentum) already score >6. Suppress social score if technicals are weak to avoid chasing meme spikes.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer already ingests Yahoo RSS news — Reddit adds the retail sentiment layer that institutional data misses. The key research insight (ACM Transactions on Social Computing 2024, Tandfonline 2024) is that WSB demonstrates collective intelligence for detecting breakout candidates, not for generating consistent alpha independently. Using it as a momentum amplifier rather than a standalone signal is the empirically correct pattern.
findings.source_evidence: Tandfonline 2024 'Democratisation of retail trading: WSB vs investment bank analysts': WSB outperformed almost all investment banks at detecting top-performing stocks over 1.6M posts / 3.5 years. AlphaArchitect (Aug 2024): BUZZ ETF -3.58% vs VOO +11.58% (Apr 2021-Mar 2024) — confirms standalone WSB signal fails. ACM 2024 WSB collective intelligence study: WSB meets Wisdom of Crowds criteria for stock selection, not directional timing.

findings.expected_impact: +2-3% win rate by requiring momentum alignment across 3 timeframes before issuing long signal; reduces false positives from single-timeframe noise
findings.summary: Implement a multi-timeframe momentum check (daily, weekly, monthly) that requires at least 2 of 3 timeframes to confirm momentum direction before the momentum dimension scores above 7. Research on time-series momentum shows that combining short-run momentum with fundamentals and multi-horizon confirmation significantly outperforms single-horizon momentum both in-sample and out-of-sample. This is practical with yfinance and directly addresses the known problem of trend signals firing during intraday noise.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the momentum dimension from single-timeframe to multi-horizon confirmation logic
findings.what_changes: For each scored symbol, compute: (1) daily momentum = price vs 20-day MA, (2) weekly momentum = weekly close vs 10-week MA from yfinance weekly data, (3) monthly momentum = price vs 3-month ROC. Count agreements. 3/3 agreement = momentum score 9-10; 2/3 = score 6-7; 1/3 = score 3-4; 0/3 = score 0-2. This replaces any single RSI/MACD momentum check with a consensus-based score.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer's paper trading results will likely show momentum signals failing during choppy markets — this filter directly addresses that. The multi-timeframe approach is the practitioner standard (price action research shows time horizons play critical roles in momentum strategy performance). It also aligns with the existing consensus-threshold architecture already built into Decifer.
findings.source_evidence: ScienceDirect 'Asset allocation with time series momentum and reversal': combining market fundamentals with multi-horizon momentum 'significantly outperforms, both in-sample and out-of-sample, the S&P 500 and pure strategies.' Oxford IMA Journal (2023): time-series momentum most effective when combined with downside risk controls across multiple rebalancing windows. Moskowitz et al. (2012) foundational: TSM based on 12-month MA persists 1-12 months then partially reverses.

findings.expected_impact: +1-2% improvement in signal consistency; eliminates dimension-scale bias where larger raw scores from some dimensions dominate composite unfairly
findings.summary: Normalise each signal dimension score to a Z-score before compositing, so that dimensions with naturally wider numeric ranges don't structurally dominate the 9-score average. The multi-factor ETH trading research (ACM 2025) found optimal Z-score thresholds of ±1.0 for buy/sell signals. Cross-sectional standardisation is a standard quant technique validated across hundreds of studies — it ensures the composite reflects genuine signal quality rather than raw scale differences between dimensions.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the composite scoring math from raw average to statistically sound Z-score normalised sum
findings.what_changes: For each dimension, maintain a rolling 60-day buffer of raw scores. At score time, Z-normalise: z = (raw_score - rolling_mean) / rolling_std. Composite = weighted_sum(z_scores) per IC weights. Apply threshold: composite Z > 1.0 = buy candidate, < -1.0 = short candidate, between = hold. This replaces the current 0-10 scale average with a statistically principled composite.
findings.why_it_matters: Without normalisation, if the news dimension routinely scores 8-10 and the momentum dimension routinely scores 4-6, the composite is biased toward news regardless of actual predictive power. Z-scoring removes this structural bias. This is low-effort, high-correctness housekeeping that professional quant shops treat as table stakes — and it's the missing mathematical foundation for IC-weighting to work properly.
findings.source_evidence: ACM 2025 'ML-Driven Multi-Factor Quantitative Model for Ethereum': optimal Z-score threshold ±1.0 maximises profitability across factor types. arxiv 2507.07107 'ML Enhanced Multi-Factor Quantitative Trading' (Jun 2025): cross-sectional normalisation described as 'critical' for strategy performance. MSCI Barra IC framework: standardised scores are prerequisite for IC calculation.
